content:
- Executed task 01 (secure explicit node config): expanded runtime config schema to include explicit API/Postgres TLS structs, API auth enum, postgres conn identity (user/dbname/sslmode), roles/auth enums, pg_hba/pg_ident sources, and DCS init payload placeholder.
- Implemented version-gated config parsing via config_version envelope; v1 uses legacy PartialRuntimeConfig + defaults; v2 parses strict v2 schema then fails closed with a clear validation error.
- Migrated all RuntimeConfig literals across src/tests/examples/harness to new schema shape; removed legacy RuntimeConfig.security and ApiConfig token fields; updated API worker to read auth + tls mode from cfg.api.security.
- Promoted pginfo module visibility so PgSslMode is reachable from public config types; added serde Deserialize for PgSslMode and made it Copy.
- Updated runtime local_postgres_dsn to use config-provided identity fields and render sslmode explicitly.
- Fixed clippy restriction failure by avoiding assert!(false, ..) and switching test to Result-returning pattern.
- Gates: make check ✅, make lint ✅, make test ✅ (200 unit tests + bdd + real-binary), make test-long ✅.
- Marked task 01 as passing and ran task_switch.sh.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md to choose next work item.
- Verified all bug tasks currently show <passes>true</passes> / <passing>true</passing> (no bug-first blockers).
- Found several unfinished (<passes>false</passes>) tasks; meta-task 18 exists but per rules unfinished tasks take priority.
- Selected next logical continuation of the active secure explicit config story: task 02 (parser/defaults/validation migration).
- Wrote selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Spawned for focused research on task 02 defaulting behavior.
- Audited src/config/defaults.rs, src/config/parser.rs, src/config/schema.rs and tests.
- Identified sensitive implicit defaults still present (postgres conn identity, roles, TLS/auth bridges via legacy security fields) and drafted explicit-enum refactor plan with validation updates.
- Next: hand findings to parent agent to fold into task plan and task file updates.

- Investigated config loading pipeline for task 02 focus area (parser/default inference of sensitive fields).
- Confirmed parser.rs itself only validates; implicit sensitive defaults come from apply_defaults on v1 path. Main hotspots: parser.rs match envelope.config_version.unwrap_or(V1), defaults.rs default_conn_identity/default_roles/tls_disabled and legacy token/tls bridge.
- Next: share exact line references + explicit-enum alternatives to parent agent for plan update and implementation.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt -> task 02 (migrate parser/defaults/validation to explicit enum-driven semantics).
- Used parallel subagents to audit `src/config/parser.rs`, `src/config/defaults.rs`, `src/config/schema.rs`, and nearby tests; confirmed sensitive inference currently comes from v1 `apply_defaults` (postgres identities/roles/pg_hba/pg_ident + legacy API security bridging) and that v2 parsing exists but is intentionally fail-closed.
- Wrote a detailed implementation plan into `.ralph/tasks/story-secure-explicit-node-config/02-task-migrate-parser-defaults-and-validation-to-explicit-enum-driven-config.md`, ending with 'TO BE VERIFIED' as instructed.
- Next: reopen task 02 and perform deep skeptical verification of the plan; must alter at least one plan decision (likely around v1 policy and/or requiring explicit config_version vs defaulting to v2) before changing marker to 'NOW EXECUTE'.

- Subagent audit focused on TOML config loading sites that could break if `config_version` becomes required.
- Searched src/tests/examples for load_runtime_config + TOML fixtures. Found TOML runtime config loads only in `src/config/parser.rs` tests (`load_runtime_config_roundtrip_and_defaults`, `load_runtime_config_rejects_invalid_file`, and existing v2 fail-closed test).
- No standalone runtime config fixture files (*.toml) exist besides Cargo/docs book manifests; examples/tests mostly build `RuntimeConfig` structs directly in Rust and are unaffected by parser-level `config_version` requirement.
- Suggested minimal-churn migration: update only parser TOML fixture strings first (explicit version line), then add one dedicated test for missing `config_version` rejection. If v1 remains executable, mark legacy fixtures as `config_version = "v1"`; if v2 is enabled in task 02, add one canonical v2 fixture and migrate only parser tests that verify parsing.
- Next if parent executes: patch parser tests accordingly and avoid broad churn across examples unless they intentionally exercise file parsing.

- Performed deep skeptical review of task 02 plan against current parser/defaults/schema code.
- Confirmed high-risk points: missing config_version currently silently defaults to v1; v2 currently fail-closed; defaults.rs still synthesizes postgres identities/roles/pg_hba/pg_ident and legacy API auth/TLS bridge.
- Proposed plan corrections: lock v1 compatibility policy (avoid accidental hard break), add explicit TLS/auth semantic validation, and split v2 input schema to allow field-path validation without exploding scope.
- Next: update task 02 markdown plan with concrete modified steps + TO BE VERIFIED/NOW EXECUTE state handling per workflow.
